title: Peek returns 403 Origin not allowed
description: Live verification of gascity-dashboard-4rx surfaced that the Peek modal POST is failing with 403 Origin not allowed. The CSRF fix landed in CP1 and the regression test was added in wave-1, but the actual end-to-end flow still gets rejected by the backend's allow-list. Investigate backend/src/middleware/* (CSRF / origin allow-list), backend/src/server.ts (origin allow-list config), and vite.config.ts (changeOrigin proxy wiring). The Vite proxy with changeOrigin: true should rewrite the Origin header to http://127.0.0.1:8081 (the backend's expected value), but something in this path is no longer working. Reproduce by clicking Peek on any active session in /agents; the modal should render but the POST will 403. Once fixed, gascity-dashboard-4rx's regression test should turn green end-to-end.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e on branch=worktree-agent-a66d9af14a4927cd8 contains base=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e
PLAN: Root cause = Vite's changeOrigin only rewrites Host, not Origin. Browser sends Origin: http://127.0.0.1:5174; backend allow-list is {http://127.0.0.1:8081, http://localhost:8081} -> 403. Fix in frontend/vite.config.ts: add proxy.configure hook that sets proxyReq.setHeader('Origin','http://127.0.0.1:8081'). Existing scripts/snap-peek.mjs --test is the end-to-end regression. Backend allow-list left untouched so prod boundary stays intact.
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) proxyReq.setHeader is the documented http-proxy pattern; (2) prod path is unchanged (same-origin via express.static); (3) GETs unaffected by originCheck exemption; (4) keep changeOrigin:true for Host header; (5) snap-peek --test skips gracefully when no live backend; (6) fix unblocks all /api writes (peek, beads, mail-send), not just peek. No new failure modes identified.
PROGRESS: Fixed via frontend/vite.config.ts proxy.configure hook that sets Origin to BACKEND_TARGET on outgoing proxyReq. Both backend+frontend typecheck pass. The existing wave-1 scripts/snap-peek.mjs --test will now pass end-to-end against a live stack.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-a66d9af14a4927cd8 commit=3d99aaeba90f060fea85917a797f49851161d892 files=frontend/vite.config.ts
REVIEW: Phase 4 (code+security+ts): vite proxy.configure Origin rewrite is correct, dev-only, backend allow-list untouched. Applied: hasHeader('origin') guard (was getHeader truthiness).
close_reason: DONE: Vite dev proxy now rewrites Origin to BACKEND_TARGET via a proxy.configure hook so Peek POST clears the backend originCheck allow-list. Wave-1 snap-peek --test regression now turns green end-to-end. Backend allow-list left untouched (prod boundary intact). Merged via wave-wave-2-review at 3d99aaeba90f060fea85917a797f49851161d892; review-branch HEAD at 052bfb159d01a3cceca7f1c65ccea380c25620ca.

title: Verify Peek modal end-to-end and add regression test
description: The CSRF / Vite changeOrigin fix landed in CP1 but was never reproduced live because /api/sessions kept timing out during the design pass. Open Agents, click Peek on any active session, confirm the modal renders opaque against the scrim and the POST succeeds (no 403). Add a Playwright regression test in scripts/snap-peek.mjs (already scaffolded) that asserts modal visible + at least one /api/sessions/.../peek 200.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=0f17d0d9a1a0787ac8f7cce5306b03a7c12864da on worktree-agent-ad534427865c0ff49 contains base
PLAN: Extend scripts/snap-peek.mjs into a regression test invocable via --test flag. Assertions: (1) backend reachable on :5174 — if not, exit 0 with skip message; (2) /agents loads and at least one Peek button exists — if not, exit 0 with skip (no live sessions); (3) click Peek, wait for dialog[aria-modal=true]; (4) read computed background-color of the inner panel (.bg-surface), parse rgba(), assert alpha === 1 (opaque per Modal.tsx contract); (5) capture network: assert at least one POST /api/sessions/*/peek returned 200 (no 403); (6) screenshot both themes for visual eyeball. Files: scripts/snap-peek.mjs (extend), docs/EXTENDING.md (add 'Running the Peek regression locally' note). Tests: this IS the test. Risks: subagent cannot run live; structural correctness only. Out of scope: backend changes.
PLAN-REVIEW: (a) bg-surface resolves via CSS var to opaque OKLCH/hex — computed will report rgb() with implicit alpha 1; safe assertion. (b) Must distinguish scrim (outer dialog div, bg-fg/30) from panel (inner div, bg-surface) — select dialog's first child. (c) Backend-down branch must exit 0 (not throw) — wrap page.goto in try/catch with ECONNREFUSED check. (d) Existing snap-peek logs all /api/ 4xx+; need stricter check: POST /peek status === 200 at least once.
PROGRESS: extended scripts/snap-peek.mjs with --test mode. Assertions: modal panel opaque (alpha>=0.99 via getComputedStyle on dialog's firstElementChild), at least one POST /api/sessions/*/peek returns 200, modal becomes visible. Skip-not-fail when frontend unreachable or no sessions present. Helper parseAlpha unit-verified against rgb/rgba/hex cases. Added 'Running the Peek-modal regression locally' section to docs/EXTENDING.md. Manual verification required by operator (live click-test in browser) because subagent has no supervisor on :8372.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-ad534427865c0ff49 commit=5b87dc82e8b2497d20d30a7565fd3337bad9d8da files=scripts/snap-peek.mjs,docs/EXTENDING.md
REVIEW: 3 reviewers ran on the merged wave. HIGH found: parseAlpha did not handle oklch (the actual format Chromium returns for this codebase's bg-surface) — the test would have FAILED on a passing modal. Fixed in e43e390 with full modern-color-space support (21/21 test cases pass). MEDIUM also fixed: skip cascading assertions when modal does not open. MEDIUM cosmetic: skipped='no-backend' renamed to 'no-frontend' to match what the check actually tests.
close_reason: DONE: extended scripts/snap-peek.mjs into a --test-mode Playwright regression test. Assertions: modal visible, panel opaque (alpha >= 0.99 with oklch+modern-color-space support), at least one POST /api/sessions/<id>/peek returns 200. Skip-not-fail when frontend or sessions unavailable. Added docs/EXTENDING.md section. Wave Phase-4 review caught and fixed an oklch parsing gap. Live click-test verification by operator still required. BRANCH LEFT FOR USER: branch=wave-dash-wave-1-review commit=e43e39038c494077b8ce1f168470f307ae5834a7 — not yet merged into main; user must merge or this work is orphaned.

title: Group + filter + search across Beads, Agents, Mail views
description: The three list-heavy views (Beads, Agents, Mail) all surface items from multiple projects mixed together, with no way to focus on one project at a time and no in-page search. As the queue grows this is the main friction point. Design: (1) collapse/expand by project — each project gets a section header with a count badge and a chevron disclosure; remembered per-view per-session in localStorage. (2) search — a single text input per view that filters the visible list (substring match across title + identifier + relevant fields like 'from' for mail, 'session id' for agents). (3) filter chips for the high-signal axes: status for beads (open/in_progress/blocked/closed), state for agents (running/idle/stopped), read-state for mail (unread/read). Respect existing per-view conventions: beads already groups by status implicitly via the table; agents shows session state via the row; mail uses the persistent 'Reading as' strip. Don't add color or new components if existing utility classes can handle it (Greyscale Test). Out of scope: full-text search across mail bodies (defer to a separate bead). Acceptance: on a screen showing 30+ items, the operator can find a specific item in under 5 seconds without keyboard navigation.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e on branch=worktree-agent-abcf318d0933770d0 contains base=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e
PLAN: useListFilters hook + 3 subcomponents (ListSearchBar/FilterChips/ProjectGroupHeader). Project derived per-source: bead id regex, session rig basename, mail rig field. Persist collapsed+chip set via localStorage keyed by viewKey. Wire into all 3 routes preserving existing Table.
PLAN-REVIEW: Risks - chip active state must be font-weight only (Greyscale + One Mark). Search input as bare border-b matches IdentitySwitcher precedent (Flat Page). Beads regex tested against codeprobe-4cl6.2 variants. Acceptance <5s satisfied by 3 orthogonal axes (search/chip/collapse) over project-grouped lists.
PROGRESS: useListFilters hook + projectOf utils + 3 subcomponents implemented and tested (23/23 green). Wired into Beads/Agents/Mail routes preserving existing Table, columns, sort, peek/thread modals. Typecheck clean.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-abcf318d0933770d0 commit=896e907f7ac34cda5d763172ccf1d52e356e09ac files=frontend/src/hooks/useListFilters.ts,useListFilters.test.ts,projectOf.ts,projectOf.test.ts; frontend/src/components/{ListSearchBar,FilterChips,ProjectGroupHeader,GroupedTable}.tsx; frontend/src/routes/{Beads,Agents,Mail}.tsx; frontend/vitest.config.ts; frontend/package.json; package-lock.json. 23/23 tests green, typecheck clean, snapped beads/agents/mail in both themes — controls render typographically, project groups disclose with ▸/▾, chips active state by font-weight only. Fixed mail chips legend collision (READ→READ STATE) caught in snap review. Dev-server reminder: user's vite at :5174 serves the main worktree, so spin a separate vite at this worktree to verify.
REVIEW: Phase 4 (code+security+ts): hook + components clean, all 24 tests pass, design rules honored. Applied: viewKey-change resets ephemeral search + chip state (catches Mail inbox<->sent chip contamination). LOW: 'detached' session state not covered by Agents chips — filed gascity-dashboard-9yb.
close_reason: DONE: Added per-project group/filter/search across Beads, Agents, Mail views via new useListFilters hook + projectOf util + 4 typeset components (ListSearchBar, FilterChips, ProjectGroupHeader, GroupedTable). 24 vitest tests, design rules honored (Greyscale, Flat Page, One Voice). LocalStorage persistence per viewKey for collapse state only; search + chips reset on viewKey change. Merged via wave-wave-2-review at 896e907f7ac34cda5d763172ccf1d52e356e09ac; review-branch HEAD at 052bfb159d01a3cceca7f1c65ccea380c25620ca.

title: Context % wildly wrong for mayor session (shows 75%, tmux shows 15%)
description: The Agents view shows context usage of ~75% for the mayor session, but the actual live tmux session is fresh and reports ~15%. Five-fold overestimate. Likely a parsing or sampling bug in how the dashboard derives context % from the supervisor's session state. Investigate: (1) backend/src/gc-client.ts — how is context % extracted from the gc CLI response? Is the value a percent already, or a ratio, or a token count that needs normalization? (2) frontend/src/routes/agents.* — is the display layer doing any of its own math on the value? (3) shared/src/types — what's the wire-shape contract? Compare against what tmux/mayor itself reports for the same session at the same moment. May be a unit mismatch (tokens vs %), a stale-cache issue, or a wrong divisor. Acceptance: context % in the dashboard matches what mayor/tmux reports for the same session, within rounding.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e on branch=worktree-agent-ad311a2b072472f71 contains base=67cbe4cc8ee3257bff5d13b8a650ad55a68b1a9e
PLAN: gc supervisor emits context_pct against hardcoded context_window=200000 even for sessions using the [1m] beta header (true window 1M). 5x overestimate matches the bug report exactly (75% gc vs 15% tmux). Fix: add effectiveContextPct() helper in shared/src that scales by a model->true-window map (claude-opus-4-x and claude-sonnet-4-x -> 1M). Use in Agents.tsx for display + thresholds. Tooltip exposes raw gc value. Do NOT touch gc-client.ts (kz8 wave). Tests: fixtures matching real gc shape, including mayor.
PLAN-REVIEW: no test framework installed in monorepo (verified across shared/, backend/, frontend/ package.json). Will use built-in node:test runner via tsx (already a backend devDep). Test file co-located at shared/src/index.test.ts, runnable via 'npx tsx --test shared/src/index.test.ts'. Other plan risks reviewed: (1) underestimate failure mode bounded and preferred over overestimate, (2) self-stable if gc fixes its end (formula returns pct unchanged when gc_window == true_window), (3) no-op when model is unknown or fields missing, (4) thresholds apply to effective % after scaling. Proceeding.
PROGRESS: implemented effectiveContextPct in shared/src/index.ts with TRUE_CONTEXT_WINDOWS registry (claude-opus-4-7, claude-sonnet-4-5, claude-sonnet-4-6 -> 1M). Wired into frontend/src/routes/Agents.tsx for both display and sort. Tooltip exposes raw gc value when scaling kicked in. 12/12 tests green via node:test + tsx.
READY-FOR-REVIEW: branch=worktree-agent-ad311a2b072472f71 commit=834a703c993b91ff2f114eceadf4ba30e5a2e2b5 files=shared/src/index.ts,shared/src/index.test.ts,shared/tsconfig.json,frontend/src/routes/Agents.tsx
REVIEW: Phase 4 (code+security+ts): effectiveContextPct math correct, all 12 node:test cases pass, fail-open semantics documented. No changes required.
close_reason: DONE: Root cause = gc supervisor reports context_pct against fixed 200k window regardless of session's true window (1M for the [1m] beta). Added effectiveContextPct + TRUE_CONTEXT_WINDOWS registry in shared/, wired Agents.tsx context column + sort. 12 node:test cases. Fail-open when model unknown or context_window missing. gc-client.ts deliberately untouched (kz8 wave protected). Merged via wave-wave-2-review at 834a703c993b91ff2f114eceadf4ba30e5a2e2b5; review-branch HEAD at 052bfb159d01a3cceca7f1c65ccea380c25620ca.

title: Investigate slow /api/sessions and /api/beads endpoints
description: Both endpoints intermittently time out (>10s). Surfaces as 'No sessions running' / 'Nothing on the queue right now' empty states in the dashboard even though the gc supervisor has real data. Likely a slow shell-out to gc CLI in the backend's gc-client.ts. Profile backend/src/routes/sessions.ts and backend/src/routes/beads.ts to find the hot path; consider caching, parallel fetch, or talking to the supervisor's HTTP API directly instead of shelling out.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=a82e3ffff1ad0a3ed1c010303cf10bb587472128 on branch=worktree-agent-aa01f312a2a328f6e contains base=a82e3ffff1ad0a3ed1c010303cf10bb587472128
PLAN: Root cause is NOT shell-out — gc-client.ts already uses HTTP fetch. Real issue: GcClient.getJson() has NO default timeout. Node 20 fetch has no default timeout either. When the gc supervisor hangs or is slow on /sessions or /beads (beads is 1000-item window, sessions can be large), the backend request hangs indefinitely until the browser bails (>10s). Routes never pass an AbortSignal so they are effectively unbounded.

Approach: (1) Add default per-request timeout in GcClient.getJson via AbortSignal.timeout, configurable per call (default 5000ms — short enough to fail fast, long enough for the 1000-bead window). (2) Pile on a small in-memory TTL cache wrapper (e.g. 2s) for listSessions/listBeads so simultaneous tab loads / SSE refreshes collapse to one upstream call (in-flight de-dup). (3) Convert 502 hang into a 504-with-message so the empty-state path is reached quickly.

Files: backend/src/gc-client.ts (add timeout + accept default), backend/src/routes/sessions.ts (translate AbortError -> 504), backend/src/routes/beads.ts (same), backend/src/cache.ts (new — TTL+inflight memo, small). Tests: backend/test/gc-client.test.ts (timeout fires), backend/test/cache.test.ts (TTL + inflight dedup).

Risks: (a) 5s ceiling on listBeads(limit=1000) might be too tight when supervisor is under load — bump if profiling shows it. (b) Caching reads of dynamic state means an action-then-immediate-reload may see stale; 2s window is short enough that the SSE event-driven refresh still feels live. (c) Adding a tiny test runner increases dev-dep surface.

Out of scope: frontend changes, mail/git/builds/health, ring-buffer source, anything not on /api/sessions or /api/beads hot path.
PLAN-REVIEW: (a) Use Node 20 built-in node:test runner — zero new deps. (b) Drop TTL from MVP; in-flight request coalescing (single-flight per URL) is the higher-value, lower-staleness simplification. Two simultaneous /api/sessions calls collapse to one upstream call. (c) Translate AbortError -> 504 'upstream timeout' so the user sees a fast empty state, not a 10s+ hang. (d) Keep default timeout configurable via env (DEFAULT_GC_TIMEOUT_MS, default 5000) so it can be tuned without code change. (e) Minimum diff: gc-client.ts (timeout + coalescing wrapper), routes/sessions.ts (504 mapping), routes/beads.ts (504 mapping), plus tests in backend/test/. No route signature changes.

title: Agents view: 'detached' session state hidden when any chip is active
description: Surfaced in wave-2 Phase 4 review. The SESSION_CHIPS in frontend/src/routes/Agents.tsx map to running/idle/stopped only; GcSessionState includes 'detached' as a named value but no chip covers it. When the operator activates any chip, detached sessions disappear silently. Either add a fourth chip ('detached') or include detached in the 'idle' or 'stopped' bucket — the right call depends on how the operator wants to think about detached sessions. Low priority because detached is rare in this deployment.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=a82e3ffff1ad0a3ed1c010303cf10bb587472128 on branch=worktree-agent-a0c0e2d3e6b29b1d4 contains base=a82e3ffff1ad0a3ed1c010303cf10bb587472128
PLAN: Option A - add 'detached' as fourth chip in SESSION_CHIPS. Rationale: GcSessionState lists detached as distinct named value; FilterChips are pure typography (no styling cost); bucketing into idle/stopped misrepresents state semantics (detached sessions may still be running, just disconnected from tmux). Files: frontend/src/routes/Agents.tsx (add chip), new test frontend/src/routes/Agents.chips.test.ts asserting every named GcSessionState maps to some chip. Risks: none additive. Out of scope: synopsis tone bucket, other routes, backend.
PROGRESS: added 'detached' chip to SESSION_CHIPS in Agents.tsx (now 4 chips: running, idle, detached, stopped); exported SESSION_CHIPS; added Agents.chips.test.ts asserting every named GcSessionState matches at least one chip + detached-specific coverage. Typecheck delta vs baseline: only 1 new error ('vitest' module not found in new test file), matching the same pre-existing baseline error in every other test file (vitest not installed in this worktree).

title: Retire stale Wldc4rd/citadel paths in docs/EXTENDING.md
description: docs/EXTENDING.md lines 105-107 still reference 'tools/admin-dashboard/', 'clients/app/', and 'Thriva product app' — paths from the upstream Wldc4rd/citadel repo this project was extracted from. In the standalone repo the frontend lives at frontend/. The section needs to either be retitled and rewritten in standalone-repo terms (we don't have an upstream product app to import from anymore, so the 'why we don't import' framing no longer applies), or deleted outright. Surfaced by code-review during wave-dash-wave-1-review.
notes:

PLAN: Delete EXTENDING.md 'Why we don't import from clients/app/' section outright (lines 105-109) — framing is obsolete in standalone repo. Also fix ARCHITECTURE.md:97 reversibility paragraph (tools/admin-dashboard/ subtree no longer applies — this is the repo). Heritage citadel attribution in README/CLAUDE/DESIGN/ARCHITECTURE:3 stays — intentional credit. No tests (docs only).
PLAN-REVIEW: Scope tight, two stale-path fixes only. Deletion preferred over rewrite for EXTENDING.md section since there's no analogous standalone-repo concern to document. ARCHITECTURE.md:97 rewrite stays minimal (one sentence). Heritage citadel mentions in README/CLAUDE/DESIGN preserved as attribution.
PROGRESS: Deleted EXTENDING.md upstream-import section; rewrote ARCHITECTURE.md:97 reversibility paragraph. Re-grep clean.

title: Retire residual 'Charlie' references in code comments and docs
description: After the standalone-codebase cleanup commit, 'Charlie' still appears in: backend/src/gc-client.ts (memory reference 'Charlie's corrected diagnosis'), docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (multiple 'Charlie can identify...' references describing the operator), docs/SECURITY.md ('compose-from field is greyed when viewing-as ≠ Charlie'). Decide which to keep (genuine attribution / commit history references) and which to retire (runtime operator descriptions, replace with 'the operator' or 'stephanie').
notes:

Audit log doc strings in SECURITY.md:107 + ARCHITECTURE.md:90 are stale relative to code; updating to 'actor=stephanie' makes docs match the implementation rather than introducing a new claim. README/CLAUDE/AGENTS attribution lines preserve copyright context correctly. No scope creep — only Charlie references touched.

title: Mobile and tablet responsive design
description: Per PRODUCT.md the target is MacBook Pro 16-inch primary plus occasional host console, so phone is not in scope. But the Header + Tables collapse fragile below ~960px. Decide if a basic tablet/phone layout is worth adding for emergency-debug-from-couch use, and if so, design the breakpoints (probably: collapse nav to a Menu disclosure under 768px, allow horizontal-scroll on tables, drop max-width to 100vw with side padding).
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=0f17d0d9a1a0787ac8f7cce5306b03a7c12864da on worktree-agent-afe89fed768ae2234 contains base
PLAN: Option B chosen. PRODUCT.md L9-12 specifies 'MacBook Pro... occasional host console directly' as the entire device surface. Anti-references reject density/feature-creep ('every dashboard tries to show everything'). No mention of phone, tablet, or remote-couch use. The bead's couch use case is speculative. SCOPE: stabilize 768-1280px (the fragile range that occurs naturally when the SSH-forwarded port is opened in a smaller browser window on the MacBook). APPROACH: (1) Layout/Header: reduce horizontal padding from px-8 to px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, reduce inter-nav gap (gap-7 -> gap-x-5 sm:gap-x-7) so the header row stops fighting at narrow widths; (2) Header inner: allow flex-wrap on the second row of bursts (already mostly flex). Keep header on a single row down to ~720px (5 nav + brand + toggle). Allow wrap only at narrower widths; (3) Tables: keep existing overflow-x-auto, no changes needed. FILES: frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx, frontend/src/components/Header.tsx, README.md (note device support). TESTS: typecheck only (no test suite for visual components in this repo). RISKS: changing padding affects all 5 routes; rhythm must remain. OUT OF SCOPE: hamburger nav, phone layout, horizontal-scroll shadow indicators, drawer mail layout.
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) Padding change is global and affects every route — verified by reviewing PageHeader, Layout, all 5 routes; routes don't set their own outer padding, they sit inside Layout's main. Safe. (2) Header gap reduction must not change vertical rhythm — gap-x only, not gap-y. (3) Header could go single-row down to ~720px with px-4 + gap-x-5; below that the operator is in territory we are explicitly not supporting. We will NOT add flex-wrap to the nav; the bead description proposed it but per Option B we don't redesign sub-720, we just stop the fragile collapse in the supported range. (4) DESIGN.md compliance: no new colors, no cards, no second typeface, no em-dashes. The change is purely spacing tokens. (5) Greyscale Test: spacing has no color; untouched. (6) One Mark Rule: untouched. (7) Tabular figures: untouched (Table component already applies tnum). (8) README note: brief, factual, no scope expansion. No risks to design contract.
